title: Earth's Systems
objective: Students will explore earth's systems concepts including Earth's atmosphere, Salt Water vs Fresh Water, Rock layers and Earth's history.

steps.content: Welcome to an exciting lesson about Earth's atmosphere!
Develop a model to describe ways the geosphere, biosphere, hydrosphere, and atmosphere interact as connected Earth systems
Let's practice together! Name the four Earth systems: geosphere (rock/land), hydrosphere (water), atmosphere (air), biosphere (living things)
Can you explain how rain (atmosphere) falls on mountains (geosphere), flows into rivers (hydrosphere), and provides water for plants and animals (biosphere)?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Earth's atmosphere: Name the four Earth systems: geosphere (rock/land), hydrosphere (water), atmosphere (air), biosphere (living things) Describe at least two interactions between different Earth systems with examples
Let's explore Salt Water vs Fresh Water! Have you ever wondered about this?
Describe and graph the amounts of salt water and fresh water in various reservoirs to provide evidence about the distribution of water on Earth
Let me show you something cool. State that about 97% of Earth's water is salt water in the oceans
Can you explain that most of Earth's water is salty ocean water, and only a tiny fraction is the fresh water we drink, and show this on a graph?

Great job! Today we learned about Salt Water vs Fresh Water. State that about 97% of Earth's water is salt water in the oceans Describe where fresh water is found: glaciers/ice caps, groundwater, rivers, lakes
Let's explore Rock layers and Earth's history! Have you ever wondered about this?

Interpret cross-section diagrams of the Earth's interior, geological strata, and rock cycle; read and label layers (crust, mantle, outer core, inner core); understand that deeper layers in sedimentary sequences are older
Time to try this out: Label the four layers of the Earth on a cross-section diagram using the correct terms
If you sees a cut-away diagram of the Earth, can they point to and name the different layers — and if shown stacked rock layers, can they say which layer formed first?

Great job! Today we learned about Rock layers and Earth's history. Label the four layers of the Earth on a cross-section diagram using the correct terms Interpret a diagram of sedimentary rock layers and identify which layer was deposited first
